what_it_does: Defines a PackageWorkspace React view for assembling a Claude Design runtime slice package, including bundle contents, editable README text, package type options, readiness checks, downstream targets, export options, and reusable package row components.
observations: Uses React state for README text and build status.
Sets ViContext to the package workspace when not embedded.
Computes readiness progress from packageState.readiness and renders pass/warn rows.
Renders a two-column workspace layout with left-side package contents and right-side readiness/downstream/export panels.
Exposes BundleRow, PkgTypeBtn, DownstreamTarget, and ExportRow on window.PkgRows for reuse.
Includes a DEFAULT_README string describing target slice, included artifacts, visual rules, exclusions, and expected outputs.
standouts: The file frames package building as a curated downstream handoff rather than a generic export screen.
The UI separates bundle contents, README authoring, readiness, recipients, and export artifacts into distinct panels.
Readiness state directly affects progress styling and the availability of sequence navigation and partial shipment controls.
conventions: Uses global React, window.MS_DATA, window.ViContext, and window.PkgRows rather than module imports/exports.
Uses small presentational row components with shared qa-row structure and Icon/Chip/Btn primitives.
Uses inline style objects heavily for layout, spacing, icon frames, and component-specific visual treatment.
Keeps package copy and README template in the same file as the workspace UI.
concerns: building state can be set to true but is not reset in this file.
Several visible buttons have no local handler, including Duplicate, row chevrons, export-row downloads, package type choices, and Have Vi write it.
The grid is fixed to two equal columns in this file, with no local responsive handling visible.
Readiness percentage divides by totalCount without a local guard for an empty readiness object.
notes: This file is a package-handoff workspace that turns packageState readiness and bundle metadata into a guided export/readiness interface.
questions: Where are Btn, Panel, Icon, Chip, QaRow, and ViNote defined?
What should complete the build/export flow after building is set to true?
Should package type buttons change state or are they static display options here?
How should the unhandled row and export buttons behave?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it builds a curated package-builder UI for downstream Claude Design handoff.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to make a design package legible, editable, and checkable before sending it to downstream agents or tooling.
novelty: The workspace treats a design handoff as a structured runtime slice package with readiness checks, downstream recipients, artifact bundles, and a narrative README template in one UI.
contribution: Contributes the package-builder surface, reusable handoff row components, readiness progress presentation, downstream target display, export artifact list, and embedded README template for a fused system to retain as handoff ledger behavior.
summary_for_embedding: PackageWorkspace is a React package-handoff screen for a Claude Design runtime slice, with bundle rows, README editing, readiness checks, downstream targets, export options, and reusable row components.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for presenting and preparing a curated visual and structural package that downstream design/prototype agents can consume.

symbols.code_id: code://counterpart-design/reference/Counterpart-handoff/counterpart/project/src/workspaces/package.jsx::BundleRow | component | function BundleRow({ icon, title, count }) {
symbols.does: Renders one bundle-content row with an icon, title, count text, and chevron button.
symbols.description: BundleRow presents a package bundle category in the Bundle contents panel. It uses the package surface styling for the icon container and displays count/detail text beneath the row title.

symbols.code_id: code://counterpart-design/reference/Counterpart-handoff/counterpart/project/src/workspaces/package.jsx::PkgTypeBtn | component | function PkgTypeBtn({ label, sub, active }) {
symbols.does: Renders a package type option button with label, description, and active outline styling.
symbols.description: PkgTypeBtn is a quiet card-like button used in the Package type panel. Its active prop changes the outline to an accent-gold treatment, while inactive options use the hairline outline.

symbols.code_id: code://counterpart-design/reference/Counterpart-handoff/counterpart/project/src/workspaces/package.jsx::DownstreamTarget | component | function DownstreamTarget({ name, status, detail, icon }) {
symbols.does: Renders one downstream recipient row with icon, name, detail text, and ready/pending chip.
symbols.description: DownstreamTarget displays a recipient of the package in the Downstream targets panel. It maps status to a success or warning Chip and labels the row as ready or pending.

symbols.code_id: code://counterpart-design/reference/Counterpart-handoff/counterpart/project/src/workspaces/package.jsx::ExportRow | component | function ExportRow({ icon, label, sub }) {
symbols.does: Renders one export option row with an icon, label, detail text, and download button.
symbols.description: ExportRow presents an available package output such as a zip, folder, share link, or screenshots. The row uses a bronze-toned icon and includes a download icon button without a local click handler.
